what_it_does: Defines a second tldraw shape type called 'forager' that renders corpus-search-hit circles on the canvas, alongside helpers to create, deduplicate, place, clear, and select those circles while keeping them separate from graph node shapes.
observations: Implements a TLBaseShape 'forager' with Circle2d geometry and HTMLContainer rendering.
Uses stable shape ids built from a djb2 hash of the corpus address prefixed with 'f-'.
Derives labels from JSON head fields when possible, falling back to the address tail.
Provides semantic zoom: expanded detail appears only when zoom > 0.5.
Hides filtered circles by setting opacity to 0 and collapsing geometry to a tiny circle.
Re-hit deduplication bumps the 'hits' prop and keys the inner div so the land animation replays.
Places new hits near the viewport center on a loose grid with deterministic per-address jitter and slot-occupancy checks.
Clear function deletes only shapes whose type is 'forager'.
useForagerSelection reads selected forager shapes directly from the editor via useValue.
Score drives a color-mix ring tint using CSS variables.
standouts: The explicit coexistence rule: type filters keep forager circles and graph nodes blind to each other.
Address-based stable shape ids make deduplication structural rather than heuristic.
Opacity-driven geometry collapse prevents selecting invisible filtered circles.
Viewport-centered deterministic placement with occupancy checking lets successive searches add legibly.
conventions: Follows the tldraw TLBaseShape + ShapeUtil + HTMLContainer + T validators pattern.
Uses CSS variable color-mix for token-derived visual styling.
Uses useEditor and useValue for editor integration.
Prefixes shape ids with a type-specific marker.
concerns: The geometry collapse relies on shape.opacity being present; its source is not shown in this file.
The placement loop has a 400-iteration guard but no explicit fallback if every slot remains occupied.
Indicator always renders at full radius even when opacity is 0 and geometry is collapsed.
Parsing head as JSON on every label computation could be expensive for large hit lists, though it is wrapped in try/catch.
notes: ForagerShape.tsx is the view-side tldraw adapter that renders corpus-search hits as stable, deduplicated circles beside graph nodes and exposes their lifecycle and selection APIs.
questions: Where is the 'forager' shape util registered with the tldraw editor?
Which component or caller invokes placeForagerHits with hits from the backend?
Where are the CSS classes forager-circle, fc-label, fc-kind, and fc-head defined?
What mechanism sets shape.opacity to 0 when a circle is filtered out?
How does indicate(address) paint the .ui-indicated ring on this disc?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements exactly the documented forager shape vocabulary, coexistence filters, deduplication, and view-side-only lifecycle.
apparent_themes: Coexistence of multiple shape vocabularies on one canvas.
View-side ephemerality: session-local search material that does not persist to the backend graph.
intention_signals: Add a second, visually distinct corpus-hit layer to the canvas that lives alongside the graph without interfering with it, using stable addressing, semantic zoom, and additive placement so search results feel like sculptable material rather than graph nodes.
novelty: A separate shape util whose geometry and lifecycle are deliberately isolated from the main node graph via type-prefix and filter rules, treating search hits as transient, address-stable circles with animated re-hit pulses.
contribution: A self-contained tldraw shape util and helper suite for rendering, placing, deduplicating, clearing, and selecting corpus-search-hit circles, plus the 'forager' type definition and address-based stable id scheme.
summary_for_embedding: ForagerShape.tsx defines the 'forager' tldraw shape, utilities for converting corpus search hits into stable circles on the canvas, and hooks for managing and selecting them without affecting graph nodes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a view-side, session-local vocabulary of corpus-search-hit circles that coexist with but remain isolated from the main graph shapes.

symbols.does: Declares the TLBaseShape props type for forager circles.
symbols.description: Defines the 'forager' tldraw shape's prop schema: width, height, corpus address, label, record kind, session, score, content head, and a hits counter used to trigger remount animations on re-hit.

symbols.does: Hashes a corpus address with djb2 to produce a stable alphanumeric string.
symbols.description: Implements a 32-bit djb2 hash over the input address and returns the result as a base-36 string, used because corpus addresses contain characters unsuitable for direct shape ids.

symbols.does: Builds a stable tldraw shape id for a forager circle from a corpus address.
symbols.description: Prefixes the djb2 hash of an address with 'f-' and wraps it in createShapeId, giving each corpus address a single, deterministic shape identity for deduplication.

symbols.does: Derives a short readable label from the tail of a corpus address.
symbols.description: Strips the URL scheme, takes the last two non-empty path segments, and truncates to 42 characters, providing a fallback visual label when the record head yields nothing meaningful.

symbols.does: Chooses a meaningful label from a hit's JSON head or falls back to the address tail.
symbols.description: Parses the hit's head JSON and prefers known meaningful fields (pattern_tag, decision, title, summary, what, purpose), then any non-empty string value, before falling back to foragerLabel so circles remain recognizable at a glance.

symbols.does: Renders and defines geometry/behavior for forager circles in tldraw.
symbols.description: Registers the 'forager' shape type with validators, default props, a circular hit area, disabled resize/rotate, semantic-zoom rendering, score-tinted ring, opacity-based hidden geometry, and a simple SVG selection indicator.

symbols.does: Types a row returned by the corpus query endpoint.
symbols.description: Describes the expected fields of a search hit: address, optional score, kind, projection, session, ts_source, and a short head string already truncated server-side.

symbols.does: Adds or updates forager circles on the canvas from a list of search hits.
symbols.description: Dedupes hits by address, updates existing circles (bumping hits to replay the land animation), and creates new ones near the viewport center on a loose, occupancy-checked grid with deterministic per-address jitter, returning counts of created and pulsed shapes.

symbols.does: Deletes all shapes of type 'forager' from the current page.
symbols.description: Collects ids of every forager shape and removes them, intentionally filtering by type so graph node shapes are never affected.

symbols.does: React hook that returns the currently selected forager shapes.
symbols.description: Subscribes to the tldraw editor's selection via useValue and filters for shapes whose type is 'forager', providing a single-source-of-truth read of selected corpus-hit circles.
